title: International Yoga Day 2026: Yoga brings people together, says PM Modi
content: This year’s theme for the International Day of Yoga is “Yoga for Healthy Ageing,” highlighting its role in promoting physical health, mental well-being, emotional resilience and active ageing

title: Crooners beat the commuting blues on a long suburban line from Chennai to Tiruvallur
content: Every day, at a set hour in the morning and evening, a compartment in the local electric train on the Chennai Central-Tiruvallur suburban line morphs into a concert hall. Listening in to the voices from this decades-old amateur train singing group, clearly a Methuselah among such groups across the Chennai Metropolitan Area

title: International Yoga Day 2026: PM Modi to lead nation from Kolkata
content: Yoga Day celebrations are being organised across nearly 2,500 locations worldwide, with participation from more than 210 Indian missions and posts

title: Indian man jailed for over 5 years in U.K.-France people smuggling case
content: Jaskirat Singh was prosecuted by the U.K.'s Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) over his role in facilitating the illegal movement of non-British nationals between December 2024 and March 2026

title: Number of patients in Bhiwandi food poisoning rises to 122
content: According to the police, several customers who had eaten food items at a local establishment on June 17 experienced stomachache and diarrhoea the following day and had to be rushed to hospitals

title: Cockroach Janta Party protest updates| Protest continues overnight; Dipke requests Delhiites to join protest on Sunday
content: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke said the protest demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan will continue

title: Insuring the informal worker against the heat
content: ‘Parametric’ schemes pay out when temperatures cross a threshold, but workers value the cash without taking the day off

title: Regular yoga practice makes healthy ageing possible: AIIMS
content: With the world’s elderly population rapidly expanding, making healthy ageing a public health priority is more critical than ever, says the AIIMS Professor

title: Employee letters disbursed under Pradhan Mantri Viksit Bharat Rojgar Yojana in Chennai
content: At IMAGE, a total of 750 participants including 450 beneficiaries, 122 employers and industry association representatives participated

title: NHAI to request Vellore Corporation to build wide culvert on Chenganatham Hill Road to prevent water stagnation
content: Areas like Rangapuram and Mullakollai, that come under ward 24 of Zone-II of Vellore Corporation, are located along the stretch

title: Congress slams Tharoor for showing ‘admiration’ for Modi; MP says statement given ‘partisan twist’
content: The row was sparked by Shashi Tharoor's reported remarks that in meetings with U.S. President Donald Trump, PM Modi conveyed that in wartime, civilian sailors on commercial ships should not be targets. Pawan Khera says Tharoor can now hear what Modi doesn’t even say.

title: GRP seize 12 kg of ganja at Jolarpet railway station
content: Initial inquiry revealed that a gang of three persons abandoned the consignment and fled the spot after they noticed a police team get onto the train

title: T.N. government opts for direct recruitment to address shortage of fireman drivers
content: Earlier, the post, which is crucial for operation of fire fighting equipment, was filled up only promotion of personnel within the service

title: India rejects Pakistan President Zardari’s remarks on Kashmir
content: Jaiswal said Pakistan's "long history of systematically targeting and victimising minorities across various faiths is notorious."

title: Pawan Kalyan bats for online citizens’ forum to question abuse of law and order, if any
content: The subject is not under my jurisdiction, but I will speak on it with the civil society, he says, and observes that criminals should not be elected to power

title: Stress takes a toll on students ahead of NEET re-exam
content: While many students are hoping that the re-exam will be held with no glitches, most point out that they have lost hope in a fair examination

title: Similipal’s black tigers are getting help
content: Zeenat, a tigress translocated from Maharashtra’s Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve to Odisha’s Similipal Tiger Reserve two years ago, has given birth to four cubs. Satyasundar Barik speaks to the people behind the high-stakes conservation effort to rescue Similipal’s tigers from a genetic mutation that has fuelled the rise of pseudo-melanism, or dense, thick, merging stripes

title: Tamil Nadu to roll out Intellectual Property Rights policy with focus on GI products
content: During the financial year 2025-26, the State had filed a total of 43 Geographical Indication registration applications; of these, 11 had been filed by the Patent Information Centre under the TNSCST; expert stresses the need to integrate GI products with the tourism sector to enhance their visibility

title: Tales TDS tells
content: Farmers in Hosur have accused the Tata Electronics plant of discharging effluents into farmlands and contaminating the water table. This report takes a deep dive to study the situation on the ground

title: Telegram | Mass messaging and more
content: The ‘WhatsApp alternative’ with a strong commitment to user privacy, which has over 15 crore users in India, is facing a week-long ban in the country

title: Lt. Gen. Dhiraj Seth | Officer at the helm
content: The new Army Chief assumes command at a juncture marked by rapid technological advances, evolving security challenges, and a growing emphasis on joint operations

title: Group of Seven | A platform for the globalised elite
content: The grouping of industrialised nations, founded in the 1970s, has weathered major geopolitical storms across the 20th and 21st centuries to remain a key forum for discussions and decision-making on global conflicts and economic challenges

title: Thenmala Punarjani shelter home case: Kerala HC expresses concern over unregistered elderly care institutions
content: HC Bench raises issue while considering a petition registered after reports of alleged ill treatment of residents at a shelter home, Punarjani in Thenmala, surfaced

title: IUML ends alliance with the DMK after six decades
content: Since the IUML has become part of the Ministry, it will cooperate with the TVK in Assembly byelections, Lok Sabha elections, and local body polls, he says

title: Engineering students urged to use technology for social transformation
content: Speaking at the 25th convocation ceremony of Seshadri Rao Gudlavalleru Engineering College, Pemmasani Chandra Sekhar encourages them to become job creators rather than job seekers; a total of 1,336 students graduate in the academic year 2025-26

title: Modi’s 12 years in office just a start: Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister lavishes praise on Prime Minister Narendra Modi, says BJP and NDA will rule the country for many years to come; highlights Centre’s role in temple developments, military modernisation and farmers’ welfare

title: Government to open international showrooms for Lepakshi handicrafts, says Minister
content: Addressing a press conference, Savitha says primary objective is to ensure a dignified and financially secure life for handloom weavers and handicraft artisans

title: Trinamool bank accounts frozen after party rebels write to police
content: Former TMC treasurer Aroop Biswas, and 10 rebel MLAs aligned with Ritabrata Banerjee, have written to the bank and the police to disallow any transaction in the party’s three accounts, which have about ₹440 crore funds

title: CID questions Trinamool veteran Sobhandeb in signature forgery case
content: He was the party’s original choice as the Leader of Opposition before a majority of MLAs sided with Ritabrata Banerjee

title: ₹47,000 crore push for Odisha as PM Modi signals strong tribal outreach
content: PM lays foundation for ₹25,016 crore coal gasification unit, ₹700 crore of rail infrastructure; says eastern India is becoming the gateway to progress; President’s in-laws’ village to be fully powered by solar power

title: Conspiracy being hatched to kill me, my father: Tej Pratap
content: Tej Pratap Yadav has lodged a police complaint against former aide Akash Yadav and the latter’s family members; he requested Bihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary to provide him with adequate security

title: 22.8 lakh to take NEET-UG re-test today across India
content: Over 22 lakh students are set to appear for the NEET (UG) 2026 re-exam nationwide. Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra lead with the highest number of candidates, while Rajasthan emerges as a significant coaching hub. A striking majority, nearly 80%, will take the test in English, with Hindi a distant second. Southern states maintain a strong presence, and regional languages see limited participation.

title: UK detains Indian captain of sanctioned ship, family in Uttarakhand pleads for release
content: An Indian captain, Ajay Pant, has been arrested in the UK and charged with breaching sanctions after his Russian oil tanker was intercepted in the English Channel. The vessel, en route to India, was found to be stateless. Pant's family in Uttarakhand has appealed to the Indian government for his safe return, while authorities are coordinating efforts for his release.

title: Yashasvi Jaiswal leaves Virat Kohli, Shubman Gill behind in major ODI record
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal etched his name in history, becoming the fastest Indian to score two ODI centuries in just six innings with a brilliant unbeaten 110 against Afghanistan. His stellar knock, coupled with Rohit Sharma's 79, guided India to a commanding nine-wicket victory and a 3-0 series whitewash.  Prasidh Krishna's five-wicket haul earlier restricted Afghanistan to 218, setting the stage for Jaiswal's record-breaking performance.

title: Protests mount in Bangladesh over alleged desecration of Hindu deity
content: Hindu communities across Bangladesh are protesting alleged government inaction following an incident where a picture of Lord Ram was reportedly desecrated. Leaders expressed deep concern over repeated attacks on minorities, emphasizing their rights as citizens and vowing to resist "evil forces" threatening religious harmony. Demonstrations, including human chains and rallies, demand swift legal action against perpetrators.

title: Bill Gates to companies building data centers: Americans won't accept half truth
content: Bill Gates has warned Amazon, Google, Meta and Microsoft that the AI datacentre buildout cannot be sold to Americans on half the truth. Some of these megacomplexes will not pay off, the Microsoft co-founder said, and no community will absorb a higher power bill to underwrite a bad bet. With 48 projects worth $156 billion already blocked in 2025 and US support at just 26 percent, the warning is no longer hypothetical.

title: Gill wins first ODI series as captain; equals Rohit Sharma's feat
content: Shubman Gill captained India to a commanding 3-0 ODI series victory against Afghanistan, marking his first series win as skipper. The young batsman also shone with the bat, finishing as the highest run-scorer with 238 runs and earning Player of the Series.  Gill praised his team's execution and highlighted the upcoming England series as crucial preparation for the 2027 World Cup.

title: 75+ Happy Father's Day messages, wishes, quotes, and greetings images for 2026
content: This Father's Day, celebrate the unwavering love and support fathers provide. They are our protectors, confidantes, and guiding lights, making every day special. The article offers heartfelt messages, wishes, and quotes to express gratitude to Indian fathers, acknowledging that showing affection can be challenging. Discover simple yet creative ways to convey your appreciation for their sacrifices and enduring presence.

title: 'We don't get to choose..': What Google CEO Sundar Pichai advices to students
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai, addressing Stanford's Class of 2026, urged graduates to embrace optimism and tackle challenging endeavors. He shared personal anecdotes, emphasizing that meaningful work stems from genuine passion rather than external expectations. Pichai's core message highlighted the importance of perseverance and continuous forward movement, regardless of life's inevitable uncertainties.

title: Shashi Tharoor's praise for PM Modi sparks political slugfest
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor's assertion that Prime Minister Modi raised concerns about Indian civilian sailors with US President Trump has sparked internal party friction.  This statement contradicts Rahul Gandhi's claim that the issue was ignored.  The BJP has seized on Tharoor's remarks, highlighting the divergence within Congress and his praise for the Prime Minister's leadership.

title: Gill breaks silence on Rohit's ODI future: 'It is a good kind of headache'
content: Shubman Gill admitted India have "a good kind of headache" over the opening combination after Yashasvi Jaiswal's second ODI hundred in three matches intensified the selection debate around Rohit Sharma ahead of the England tour. While backing Jaiswal's talent, Gill said the final XI would depend on the squad and players' fitness.

title: UK PM Keir Starmer expected to announce resignation on Monday: Report
content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is reportedly poised to announce his resignation on Monday, following mounting pressure after Andy Burnham's significant by-election win.  Discussions with key figures suggest Starmer is considering a departure timetable.  While he previously vowed to fight any challenge, allies of Burnham and Wes Streeting are urging a smooth transition.  Starmer has been assessing his support within his cabinet.

title: White House clears rule limiting status of foreign students in US that many have opposed
content: The White House has greenlit a new rule from the Department of Homeland Security that will end the open-ended stay for foreign students, exchange visitors, and media representatives.  Instead, these individuals will now receive a fixed four-year admission period, requiring them to renew their status. This change aims to reduce overstays and enhance national security, though critics warn of administrative burdens.

title: How to use Roblox sound IDs with the megaphone in Grow a Garden 2 - easy guide
content: Grow a Garden 2 players can now inject fun and chaos into their gameplay with the affordable Megaphone gear. This item allows users to broadcast any Roblox Sound ID across the server, offering endless entertainment and trolling opportunities.  Acquiring the Megaphone is simple, costing just 8,000 Sheckles.  Discover popular sound IDs like 'Raining Tacos' and 'Tokyo Drift' to liven up your virtual world.

title: Fortnite Gummy Sprite Power Hour June 20: Start times across all regions
content: Fortnite fans, get ready for Gummy Hour Power Hour this Saturday, June 20, 2026! Epic Games is doubling the fun with two extended two-hour sessions, offering a boosted chance to snag elusive Gummy Sprites. Players will also benefit from a Self-Revive Device, regenerating health after shields break, and earn double Sprite Dust from extractions. Don't miss this prime opportunity to enhance your collection.

title: Georgina Rodriguez stuns on luxury Yacht as Ronaldo faces World Cup pressure
content: While Cristiano Ronaldo battled scrutiny on the field in Houston, Georgina Rodriguez drew attention far from the World Cup spotlight. The model and entrepreneur posted images aboard a luxury yacht and continued promotional work for Harper’s Bazaar Spain, remaining in Portugal as her fiancé endured a frustrating opening match at what could be his final FIFA World Cup.

title: FSSAI targets misleading ads as brands race to sell ‘healthy’ food
content: India's food regulator, FSSAI, is cracking down on misleading health claims on food and supplement labels. Products marketed as "natural," "healthy," or "no added sugar" are under scrutiny for potentially deceiving consumers. This intensified enforcement follows rising complaints and social media attention, aiming to ensure transparency and protect public health by urging consumers to look beyond catchy marketing phrases.

title: At Lahore's 'Eton', Pakistani alum pays tribute to Indian dost
content: A classroom at Lahore's Aitchison College, once attended by leaders from undivided Punjab, now bridges India and Pakistan.  A tribute to former Punjab CM Harcharan Singh Brar, funded by his 100-year-old schoolmate Syed Babar Ali, highlights enduring friendships forged before Partition. This initiative is part of a larger effort to remember the institution's pre-1947 past, honouring students and teachers scattered by history.

title: CBSE to consult key stakeholders on digital marking system
content: CBSE's on-screen marking system for Class XII exams faces an uncertain future. The board will consult students, teachers, and parents before deciding on its continuation for 2027 and potential expansion to Class X.  This review follows complaints about the system's debut, including scanning issues and unevaluated answers, and aims to address gaps exposed during recent exams and rebuild trust.

title: Indian, Pak Army officers shake hands during a repatriation process along LoC
content: In a gesture of goodwill, the Indian Army repatriated a Pakistani national, Asad Khan, apprehended along the Line of Control in Kupwara last week. Khan, who reportedly crossed the LoC inadvertently while seeking employment, was treated with dignity and compassion. The handover at the Aman Setu peace bridge signifies a potential easing of tensions, underscoring the Indian Army's commitment to humanitarian values and professionalism.

title: Row erupts over Vijay songs, TVK campaign music at Coimbatore medical college convocations
content: Convocation ceremonies at ESI and Coimbatore Medical Colleges became a point of contention as film songs featuring actor Vijay and ruling TVK election campaign tunes blared.  "Raavana Mavanda" and "Oru Pere Varalaru" from Vijay's upcoming film, alongside TVK campaign anthems, were played, drawing criticism for a perceived lack of discipline at government events. Netizens expressed dismay, calling the administration a "joke."

title: Ladakh groups accuse Centre of reneging on agreement, call shutdown
content: Ladakh's political groups, Leh Apex Body and Kargil Democratic Alliance, have called for a shutdown on June 23, accusing the Centre of reneging on agreements regarding legislative powers and constitutional safeguards. They warn of renewed agitation if their demands for full statehood and Sixth Schedule status are not met, emphasizing that public mobilization will proceed despite the Dalai Lama's upcoming visit.

title: Kheer Bhawani yatra begins; 8,500 pilgrims in 214 buses leave Jammu
content: Over 8,500 pilgrims, predominantly Kashmiri Pandits, embarked on their journey from Jammu for the annual Kheer Bhawani Mela.  The significant increase in registrations this year is attributed to a peaceful environment, enhanced security, and comprehensive arrangements. Officials have been stationed at various shrines across Kashmir to ensure smooth proceedings for the devotees participating in the revered yatra.

title: Hum do, hamare 1.9: What India’s falling fertility rates reveal
content: India, once concerned about a population boom, now faces a declining birth rate, with the Total Fertility Rate dropping to 1.9 children per woman, below the replacement level. This significant shift reflects women's increased educational attainment, career focus, and later marriages, leading to more personal choices about family planning. The narrative is evolving from population control to individual decisions, marking a societal transition.

title: With eyes on 2027 polls, BJP chief Nitin Nabin begins Punjab tour
content: BJP chief Nitin Nabin launched a three-day Punjab visit, emphasizing a vision for the state mirroring Maharaja Ranjit Singh's era. His tour, including prayers at the Golden Temple and a Jalandhar roadshow, signals intensified preparations for the 2027 Assembly elections. Nabin met with the Punjab BJP core committee to strategize on organizational strengthening and booth-level expansion, aiming to form a 'double-engine government' under PM Modi's leadership.

title: Petrol, diesel rates may ease as cheaper crude arrives, says oil minister Puri
content: Petrol and diesel prices may soon see a dip as India receives cheaper crude oil, Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri indicated. He defended domestic fuel pricing, stating increases have been minimal globally despite market volatility. The government has absorbed significant costs through excise duty cuts, shielding consumers from the full impact of rising international rates. Puri also highlighted Sonbhadra's development progress.

title: The EV dilemma: Why Delhi’s mechanics and dealers are wary of going electric
content: Delhi's bustling used car market faces a seismic shift as electric vehicles gain traction. While mechanics struggle with EV repairs, citing complex systems and lack of training, the city's push for cleaner mobility is evident. Dealers grapple with battery concerns and resale challenges, as the automotive ecosystem slowly adapts to the electric future, leaving many traditional players uncertain about their place.

title: 18-year-old drugged, recorded, raped in Gujarat
content: A young woman has accused three individuals of rape and blackmail after allegedly being drugged and filmed unconscious. The main accused, Dipak, is reported to have used the nude video to coerce her into multiple sexual assaults and a forced marriage. He also allegedly threatened her with extreme violence to ensure compliance. Police have arrested one suspect, while Dipak remains at large.

title: 'Yeh dil maange more': How Captain 'Sher Shah' led capture of Point 5140, 27 years ago
content: Indian Army achieved a pivotal victory at Point 5140, the highest point on the Tololing ridgeline, on June 20, 1999. This strategic capture, led by Captain Sanjeev Singh Jamwal and Captain Vikram Batra, crucial for controlling the Dras sector and the Srinagar-Leh National Highway, was announced with distinct success signals. The triumph paved the way for further Indian advances, marking a turning point in the Kargil War.

title: Delhi road rage: BBA students chase man’s car, beat him up for 'not giving way'
content: A Delhi man was injured after a road rage incident on Sohna Road. Two men in an SUV allegedly chased his car, smashed its windows at a traffic signal, and attempted to assault him following a dispute over overtaking. Police swiftly arrested two 22-year-old BBA graduates, Parth and Paras, who admitted to the attack after the victim refused to yield. Their Scorpio vehicle has been recovered.

title: 'Heard what PM Modi didn't say': Congress taunts Shashi Tharoor; he claps back with 'I read widely'
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor defended his remarks praising PM Modi's handling of the civilian sailors' issue, stating he relied on widely published reports. This followed criticism from colleague Pawan Khera, who questioned Tharoor's claims, citing a lack of mention in official statements. The BJP also weighed in, highlighting perceived internal Congress divisions over the matter.

title: Happy Father’s Day: Wishes, messages, quotes, images, Facebook and WhatsApp status
content: Father's Day 2026 offers a vital opportunity to acknowledge the quiet strength and unwavering support fathers provide.  It's a day to recognize their practical love, sacrifices, and the steady presence that shapes lives.  Beyond traditional celebrations, the occasion encourages expressing gratitude for guidance, resilience, and love in all its forms, ensuring these often-unheard heroes feel truly appreciated.

title: Root scripts history, becomes only Test batter after Tendulkar to...
content: England’s Joe Root became only the second batter after Sachin Tendulkar to surpass 14,000 Test runs, reaching the milestone during the second Test against New Zealand at The Oval. The 36-year-old achieved the feat in his 302nd innings and now ranks second on the all-time run-scoring list, further cementing his status among Test cricket’s greatest batters.

title: Cancer patient sexually abused inside Haryana police station, 3 cops suspended
content: Following alarming claims from a 30-year-old cancer survivor, Kurukshetra has suspended two Assistant Sub-Inspectors and a Home Guard. The man alleges assault and sexual violence at a police station after a minor conflict. An FIR is under investigation by a Special Investigation Team, focusing on serious charges of sodomy. The complainant’s health and collaboration with the inquiry are being closely monitored amidst this sensitive case.

title: 3 Indian-flagged oil tankers cross Strait of Hormuz, head home with 94 crew members
content: Three Indian oil tankers, carrying over 8.6 lakh metric tonnes of cargo and 94 crew members, have successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz and are heading home. This safe passage follows a US decision to lift its blockade on Iran, allowing resumed movement through the vital waterway. The government assures continued efforts to protect India's maritime and energy interests, with the vessels expected to reach Indian ports next week.

title: CJP presses for Pradhan’s resignation at second Jantar Mantar protest
content: Despite police calls to evacuate after the 5 p.m. deadline, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke and many protestors stayed back and said he would continue to stay until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigned.

title: NEET-UG 2026: Over 6,000 observers to ensure ‘fair, secure’ retest on June 21, NTA says
content: 22 lakh aspirants likely to attend the exam after previous test was cancelled over paper leak allegations; CCTV, jammers installed at all exam centres; additional staff and invigilators deployed

title: Hyderabad could see large-scale deletions in SIR owing to residents shifting homes
content: Enumeration forms will be printed in English for the city voters, following request from all the political parties.

title: Bengal free of shackles and on path to creating history, says PM Modi
content: The Prime Minister, who participated in an event to mark Paschim Banga Dibas in Hooghly’s Tarakeswar, said that during partition attempts were made to include the whole of Bengal in Pakistan, and accused the Congress party of surrendering before those conspirators

title: Monsoon tracker June 20 highlights | IMD warns of isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall in several regions
content: Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall likely over sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and northeast India during the week with possibility of isolated extremely heavy rainfall over sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim till June 21

title: Seven dead, 25 injured in roof collapse at temple in Maharashtra’s Parbhani
content: The rescue operations continued for two and a half hours; cause of the collapse is under investigation

title: ‘Biotechnology plays a vital role in human well-being, sustainable development’
content: ‘Restoration and conservation of plant and animal resources should be a priority if the world is to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals set by the United Nations’

title: Bangladeshi among nine wildlife traffickers held in Assam
content: One of the eight golden langurs recovered from their possession was found dead. Officials say the animal is an endangered species with a blackmarket value of about ₹16 lakh, adding that the seized animals will be released in their natural habitat soon

title: No safety lapse at Chirakkal gate, says DRM
content: Divisional Railway Manager Madhukar Roat said that the Mangaluru-Chennai Mail (Train No. 12602) stopped before the level-crossing signal as it was not cleared

title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: JD Vance On His Way To Switzerland For Iran Talks
content: US-Iran Peace Talks Live Updates: JD Vance had on Thursday cancelled his trip to Switzerland amid rising tension in Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

title: Over 22 Lakh Students, NTA Set For High-Stakes NEET Re-Exam Today
content: The re-test will be held from 2 pm to 5.15 pm. Candidates with disabilities, who are eligible for compensatory time, will be allowed time till 6.20 pm.

title: 'He Hears What PM Doesn't Say': Congress, Shashi Tharoor In Fresh Bout
content: Pawan Khera took up the mantle to chastise his senior colleague. His message, though sarcastic, conveyed the party's stern stance on Tharoor, drawing a sharp response from the MP.

title: Israeli Army Confirms Journalist Killed In Gaza, Calls Him 'Hamas' Terrorist
content: The Israeli military said Saturday it had carried out a strike that killed Al Jazeera journalist Ahmed Wishah in Gaza

title: Woman Stabbed To Death, Husband Injured In Fight Over Parking In Delhi
content: During preliminary inquiry, the police found that the couple had got into a fight with their neighbours over the parking of a motorcycle on the road.

title: 'Jai Shri Ram' Chants In Dhaka As Thousands Protest 'Desecration' Of Image
content: Thousands of Hindus gathered for a torchlight procession in Dhaka on Friday, chanting "Jai Shri Ram" and demanding the arrest of those accused of insulting the image.

title: Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz Again Over Israel's Attacks On Lebanon
content: Iran said that the closure was the "first step" in response to what it described as breaches of commitments.

title: How Trump's New Plane Compares With His Private Jet, Old Air Force One
content: The new aircraft is a 747-8i jumbo jet that was modified as a VVIP business liner for the Qatari royal family.

title: Hands Tied, Posing Before Grill: Cops Make Surat Man Reenact Fake Kidnapping
content: Investigation disclosed that the man had lost between Rs 50 lakh and Rs 60 lakh in the stock market and options trading and was heavily in debt.

title: Iranian Crude Loading Resumes At Kharg Island After US Blockade Lifted
content: Three very large crude carriers, each capable of hauling about 2 million barrels of crude, are moored at the Sea Island terminal to the west of Kharg, according to ship tracking data compiled by Bloomberg.

title: PM Modi Releases PM-Kisan Instalment Worth Rs 18,880 Crore In Bengal
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi released the 23rd instalment of the PM-Kisan scheme worth Rs 18,880 crore, benefiting over 9.44 crore farmers across the country, while unveiling various development projects in the state.

title: 'Thinking Of Doing Something Different': UP Medical Student Dies Of Suicide
content: According to police, the victim, identified as Jatin Kumar, was a resident of H-Block in Vijay Nagar and had been preparing for the medical entrance examination.

title: "My Popularity None Of Your Concern": Trump-Meloni Photo War Escalates
content: In a post on X, US President Donald Trump said that Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni asked him for a photo "over and over" during the G7 Summit in France. He claimed that Meloni is resorting to these tactics because she is "doing poorly" in Ital

title: 'Vigilant To Ensure Deal Obeyed': US Military After Iran Shuts Hormuz
content: Iran had announced moments earlier that it was closing the strait in response to Israeli strikes on Lebanon, deeming them a violation of its fragile deal with the United States.

title: Iran Negotiators Leave For Switzerland To Discuss Peace Deal With US
content: An Iranian negotiating team left Iran for Switzerland on Saturday for discussions on the implementation of the US-Iran agreement.

title: Maharashtra To Centre: How 'Op Tiger' May Affect Eknath Shinde's Stature
content: Ever since the Mahayuti's victory in the Maharashtra Assembly elections in 2024, there has been a perception that Shinde's influence had diminished.

title: "Which Tribe?" JD Vance's Mother Was Confused About Usha's Indian Origin
content: In the podcast, US Vice President JD Vance said that when he told his mother that Usha was an Indian, she asked, "Which tribe?"

title: Mob Mistakes NGO Worker For Kidnapper In Odisha Village, Thrashes, Strips Her
content: The woman and her colleague talked to children and distributed biscuits. Soon, a rumour spread in the village that they were child kidnappers.

title: America@250 swathed in red, white, and blue — and green algae
content: the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in the us capital was designed to mirror America's greatness . instead, it is currently redolent of an expensive middle-school science experiment that has gone spectacularly wrong . the president's vision was straightforward enough .

title: Garudastra, India’s heaviest 120-mm vehicle-mounted mortar system, successfully tested
content: india recently tested its heaviest indigenous 120-mm vehicle-mounted mortar system, ‘Garudastra’, in Madhya Pradesh . Designed for high mobility and precision, the system successfully completed live demonstrations for the Indian Army .

title: PM Modi to commission 2 warships into Navy
content: the Indian Navy is scheduled to commission three indigenously-built frontline platforms . the platforms will bolster India's blue-water capability, shallow-water anti-submarine operations, and oceanographic surveying . GRSE was granted the 'Navratna' status on Friday .

title: Jammu & Kashmir suspends cardiologist over ‘false’ Ayushman-Bharat claims
content: an associate professor at Govt Medical College in anantnag allegedly recorded 103 advanced pacemaker procedures under an Ayushman Bharat PMJAY category that did not cover them . the cardiologist was suspended over allegations that he recorded "left bundle branch area pacing" procedures as dual-chamber pacemaker implantations .

title: Modi a tough cookie, 1 of my 2 favourite world leaders: Trump
content: 'i think Modi is very good. Yeah... he stays out of wars, which is smart,' he says . "he's been there for more than 12 years... very solid... there's a great calmness," he adds . he also cites asim Munir and shehbaz Sharif as leaders whom he liked .
